events.title: The Contract on the Table
events.scene: The offer is real: a professional contract, your name on a roster, the chance to test the gift against the best in the world. But a body is a wasting asset, and the smart money says the real fortune is in OWNING the game, not playing it. The {surname} line stands at the fork between the field and the front office.
events.researchNote: EX-3 athletic-locked beat: the player-vs-owner fork that decides whether the dynasty is built on prowess or on the business of sport.

choices.text: Take the field — chase the records, the rings, the roar
choices.outcome: You play, and you win, and a generation learns the {surname} name from the back of a jersey. Fame arrives before the fortune does.

choices.text: Buy in instead — own the team, the venue, the franchise
choices.outcome: You decide the smart play is the front office. The {surname} line will collect from every gate, every broadcast, every athlete who ever takes its field.

events.title: A League of Your Own
events.scene: An upstart league needs an owner with nerve, or an established team can be bought if you overreach. Sports franchises are vanity, leverage, and a megaphone all at once — a way to put the {surname} name on every sports page in the country. The debt is real; so is the spotlight.
events.researchNote: EX-3 athletic+economic beat: the franchise as brand-and-leverage play (parallels the repurposed USFL trope).

choices.text: Bankroll the upstart league and dare to topple the establishment
choices.outcome: You bet against the giants. Win or lose, the {surname} name is suddenly in every sports section in the country — exactly as intended.

choices.text: Buy an established, beloved team and inherit its goodwill
choices.outcome: You purchase a city's love along with its team. The {surname} name is cheered on opening day before it has done a thing to earn it.
